text: Fill in the blanks on central nervous system physiology
questions: The human brain contains approximately *86/eighty-six* billion neurons.
The functional hierarchy of the brain from oldest to newest is: brainstem, diencephalon, cerebellum, basal ganglia, limbic system, and *cerebral cortex*.
The *thalamus* acts as the relay station for all sensory signals (except smell) before they reach the cerebral cortex.
The *hypothalamus* is the master regulator of homeostasis, controlling body temperature, hunger, thirst, and the autonomic nervous system.
Upper motor neuron (UMN) lesions cause *spastic/spasticity* paralysis with hyperreflexia, while lower motor neuron (LMN) lesions cause flaccid paralysis with hyporeflexia.
The *reticular activating* system in the brainstem is responsible for maintaining consciousness and wakefulness.
In Parkinson's disease, there is depletion of *dopamine* in the substantia nigra of the basal ganglia.
The Gate Control Theory of pain states that activation of large-diameter *A-beta* fibres can inhibit pain transmission by small C fibres at the spinal cord level.
*Wernicke's/Wernicke* area in the superior temporal gyrus is responsible for language comprehension.
Brown-Sequard syndrome results from *hemisection/lateral hemisection* of the spinal cord, causing ipsilateral motor loss and contralateral pain/temperature loss.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
